result:
Done. All three missing operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr`, the project builds cleanly, and every behavioral check passes.

What I added (in Calor, following the existing `NeedsQuoting` style):

- **`EscapeField`** — calls `NeedsQuoting`; when true, doubles internal quotes with `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps in quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim. Pure (`§E{}`).
- **`JoinRow`** — index loop over the list, escaping each field and inserting commas between elements; the empty list yields the empty string. Pure.
- **`SplitRow`** — a character-scanning state machine with an `inq` (in-quotes) flag: doubled quotes inside a quoted field contribute one literal quote, a lone quote closes the field, commas outside quotes end fields, and the accumulated field is pushed once more at end of input (so `""` parses to `[""]` and a trailing comma yields a trailing empty field). Declares `§E{mut}` since it pushes into a list.

One signature wrinkle: `[str]` in Calor maps to `string[]`, but the spec pins `List<string>`, so `JoinRow`/`SplitRow` use `List<str>` (confirmed via the compiler's `TypeMapper`).

Verification used 2 of the 10 build cycles plus a throwaway console runner in /tmp (deleted afterward — no test files were added to the project). It exercised the round-trip `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` on 11 field lists covering commas, quotes, doubled quotes, CR/LF content, empty fields, and trailing empties — all passed — plus the specified edge cases: `JoinRow([])` returns `""`, `SplitRow("")` returns a single empty field, and `NeedsQuoting` behavior is unchanged.
